rationale: The spec is close, and most ACs are runnable with PASS-line checks for named new tests. However, the falsifiability bar is not met for all claimed behavior. The doctor walk-count AC requires an exact WalkDir count, but current code calls filepath.WalkDir directly and the spec does not say to add an injectable walk/list seam. Several byte-identical claims are broader than the golden tests required: only trace NDJSON and the two approve writes have explicit golden proofs, while validate ADR diagnostics, ownership attribution diagnostics, doctor warn/pass output, and contextpack budgeting are claimed unchanged without equivalent golden/fixture-output ACs. R7 also names three ownership-load hoisting call sites, but the AC only proves the divergence path. These gaps leave implementers room to satisfy the listed commands while missing specified invariants.
concrete_changes_required: Specify the doctor walk-count seam explicitly, for example a package-level walk/list function or helper whose calls can be counted in TestOwnershipCheckWalksTreeOnce, or change the AC to a runnable proof that does not require an absent seam.

Add acceptance criteria or narrow the claims so every byte-identical behavior promise has a golden/fixture-output proof, especially ADR validation diagnostics, ownership attribution diagnostics, doctor ownership check output, and contextpack budgeting output.

Expand the ownership-load perf ACs to cover all R7 call sites named in the requirement: ValidateDivergence, checkInternalPackages, and normalizeImpactedDomains, or scope R7 down to the single tested path.
Fix the carve-out AC command to assert validateReviewMode remains as well as findRoot, matching the parenthetical requirement text.

findings.detail: internal/doctor/ownership.go calls filepath.WalkDir directly inside manifestResolvesAny. The spec requires a walk-counting seam test proving exactly one workspace walk, but it neither identifies an existing seam nor instructs implementers to add one.

R2 and R4 have golden-test ACs, but the broader byte-identical claims for ADR validation outcomes, ownership attribution findings/errors, doctor warn/pass results, and contextpack budgeting output lack golden or fixture-output acceptance criteria.

R7 requires hoisting loads in ValidateDivergence, checkInternalPackages, and normalizeImpactedDomains, but the only counting AC is TestDivergenceOwnershipLoadedPerDomainNotPerFile. The other two required hot paths can regress while ACs still pass.
The AC text says the live findRoot carve-out and validateReviewMode carve-out remain untouched, but the command only greps for findRoot after removing brownfield/plan and isNeighbor.
